LAW AND MOTION 302, RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES, WHICH IS UNOPPOSED, IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: JOSEPH QUINN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-25-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation Of The Parties
Off Calendar Jun-12-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation Of The Parties is Granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown.

Order and Judgment are signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/HEK)

Off Calendar Jan-29-2025 Continued To Apr-30-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of And Motion For Ordersetting Aside Dismissal Of The Case
Off Calendar Oct-16-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. ROSEMARY MCCARTHY, Esq. (tel: 408-569-5891) specially appearing for Plaintiff Bank of America, N.A.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s Motion For Order setting Aside Dismissal Of The Case.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's unopposed "motion for order setting aside dismissal of the case" is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-12-2024 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance or objection filed, the Court orders the case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Proceedings not reported. (610)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-14-2024 Continued To Jun-11-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of And Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation Of The Parties
Off Calendar Oct-24-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Plaintiff's Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation Of The Parties.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Bank of America, N.A.'s motion for entry of judgment pursuant to stipulation of the parties, which is unopposed, is GRANTED.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-11-2019 Continued To Apr-09-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
